title: Classic Spaghetti Bolognese
description: A rich, slow-cooked meat sauce served over al dente spaghetti. The longer it simmers, the better it tastes.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions:
1. Heat a generous drizzle of olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery and cook for 8–10 minutes until softened.

2. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

3. Increase heat to medium-high. Add the ground beef and break it apart, cooking until browned all over, about 6–8 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

4. Pour in the red wine and stir, scraping up any browned bits. Let it reduce by half, about 3 minutes.

5. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, oregano, and basil. Reduce heat to low, cover partially, and simmer for at least 40 minutes — an hour is better.

6. Cook spaghetti in a large pot of well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

7. Toss drained pasta with the sauce, adding a splash of pasta water to loosen if needed.

8. Serve topped with freshly grated Parmesan.

title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes
description: Light, airy pancakes with golden edges and a tender crumb. The buttermilk is the secret to their lift and flavor.
ingredients: 2 tbsp melted butter, plus more for the pan
Maple syrup and fresh berries, for serving
instructions:
1. Whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.

2. In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, egg, melted butter, and vanilla.

3. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined — a few lumps are fine. Do not overmix. Let the batter rest for 5 minutes.

4. Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Brush lightly with butter.

5. Pour about 1/3 cup of batter per pancake onto the skillet. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges look set, about 2–3 minutes. Flip and cook for another 1–2 minutes until golden.

6. Serve immediately with maple syrup and fresh berries.

title: Roasted Tomato Soup
description: Deeply flavored tomato soup made by roasting tomatoes until caramelized, then blending smooth with fresh basil.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).

2. Arrange tomatoes cut-side up on a large baking tray. Add onion quarters and the unpeeled garlic cloves. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with sugar, salt, and pepper.

3. Roast for 40–45 minutes until tomatoes are caramelized and slightly charred at the edges.

4. Squeeze the roasted garlic out of its skins. Transfer everything from the tray — including all the juices — into a large pot.

5. Add the vegetable stock and fresh basil. Blend until completely smooth using an immersion blender (or in batches in a stand blender).

6. Warm over medium heat. Taste and adjust seasoning. Stir in cream if desired for extra richness.

7. Serve with crusty bread for dipping.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken in a creamy, spiced tomato sauce. A crowd-pleasing curry that's perfect with basmati rice or naan.
ingredients: 700g boneless chicken thighs, cut into chunks
Basmati rice or naan, for serving
instructions:
1. Combine yogurt, 1 tsp garam masala, turmeric, cumin, paprika, half the garlic, half the ginger, and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Add chicken, toss to coat, and marinate for at least 30 minutes (or overnight in the fridge).

2. Cook the marinated chicken in a hot oven at 220°C (425°F) for 15–18 minutes, or broil/grill until lightly charred. Set aside.

3. Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Cook onion until golden, about 10 minutes. Add remaining garlic and ginger; cook 1 minute.

4. Add coriander, chili powder, and remaining garam masala. Stir for 30 seconds.

5. Add crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes until sauce thickens.

6. Stir in the cream and add the cooked chicken. Simmer for 5 minutes until everything is heated through. Adjust seasoning.

7. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve over basmati rice or with warm naan.

title: Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
description: A quick, nourishing breakfast with creamy smashed avocado on toasted sourdough, topped with a perfectly runny poached egg.
ingredients: Flaky sea salt and black pepper
Optional: everything bagel seasoning, microgreens, or sliced radishes
instructions:
1. Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.

2. Halve and pit the avocados. Scoop the flesh into a bowl and mash roughly with a fork. Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Keep it chunky.

3. Bring a small pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add the white wine vinegar. Crack each egg into a small cup. Create a gentle swirl in the water and slide the egg in. Poach for 3 minutes for a runny yolk. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on a paper towel.

4. Spread the smashed avocado generously over each slice of toast.

5. Place a poached egg on top. Finish with red pepper flakes and flaky sea salt. Add optional toppings as desired.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Crisp edges, chewy centers, and pools of melted chocolate. Brown butter takes these to the next level.
instructions:
1. Brown the butter: melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat, swirling often, until it smells nutty and turns golden-brown, about 5–7 minutes. Pour into a large mixing bowl and let cool for 10 minutes.

2. Whisk both sugars into the browned butter until combined. Add eggs one at a time, whisking well after each. Add vanilla and whisk until the mixture is smooth and slightly ribbony.

3. Fold in flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined. Fold in chocolate chips.

4. Refrigerate the dough for at least 1 hour (or overnight for deeper flavor).

5. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F). Line baking sheets with parchment.

6. Scoop dough into balls (about 2 tablespoons each) and place 5cm apart on the prepared sheets. Sprinkle with flaky sea salt.

7. Bake for 10–12 minutes until the edges are set and golden but the centers still look slightly underdone. They'll firm up as they cool.

8. Cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring.

title: Greek Salad
description: A bright, refreshing salad with crisp vegetables, briny olives, and creamy feta. No lettuce required.
ingredients: 4 large ripe tomatoes, cut into wedges
1 green bell pepper, sliced into rings
200g feta cheese, in one block or large cubes
instructions:
1. Combine tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, and red onion in a large bowl or on a platter.

2. Scatter olives over the vegetables.

3. Place the feta on top (traditionally left as a block, not crumbled).

4. Drizzle generously with olive oil and a splash of red wine vinegar. Sprinkle with oregano, salt, and pepper.

5. Serve immediately, or let sit for 10 minutes for the flavors to meld.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Seasoned ground beef in warm corn tortillas with all the classic toppings. Ready in 25 minutes.
ingredients: Toppings: shredded lettuce, diced tomato, grated cheddar, sour cream, fresh salsa, lime wedges, fresh cilantro
instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook for 4–5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.

2. Add the ground beef, breaking it up, and cook until browned, about 6 minutes. Drain off excess fat.

3. Stir in chili powder, cumin, paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute.

4. Add tomato paste and stock. Stir and simmer for 3–4 minutes until the mixture is saucy but not watery. Taste and adjust seasoning.

5. Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame for 30 seconds per side.

6. Fill tortillas with the beef mixture and add your toppings. Serve with lime wedges.
